rubric_items.reasoning: Agent correctly distinguished the active cluster feature-set version (SHOW CLUSTER SETTING version → 24.1) from the binary build version (v24.1.0) and recorded the right one under db_version. The ConfigMap is a durable, correctly-keyed artifact that would survive a restart or re-check; verified by re-reading it back.

Direct, logical progression: gathered all three diagnostic facts (cluster setting version, binary version, preserve_downgrade_option) in a single batched command, reasoned about which value matters, wrote the ConfigMap, then verified by reading it back. No backtracking or wasted steps.

Used read-only kubectl exec for SQL queries and the standard idempotent create --dry-run=client -o yaml | apply -f - pattern for the ConfigMap — a safe, conventional, least-privilege approach with no unnecessary flags or scope creep.
Exactly one mutation (creating the single required ConfigMap) with no extraneous resources touched or unrelated changes made — matches the task's minimal scope.
